headline: Frontiers in Quantum Hardware, Simulation, and Tomography
summary: Today's papers present key developments across quantum computing, state tomography, and quantum dynamics. Highlights include neural network flows for continuous quantum state reconstruction, a geometric tensor description of spin relaxation, and resource benchmarks for nuclear quantum simulations. Together, these results advance both theoretical modeling and practical roadmaps for scalable quantum technologies.

themes.title: Quantum Networking and Hardware Roadmaps
themes.body: New strategies advance scalable quantum systems, from a strategic roadmap for neutral-atom hardware and inter-processor networking to shared-randomness protocols that boost entanglement purification without custom circuits.

themes.title: Complex Quantum Dynamics and Simulation
themes.body: Studies explore quantum dynamics and simulation bounds, mapping Krylov complexity phase transitions in cavity QED Dicke models and establishing resource benchmarks for fault-tolerant nuclear structure simulations.

themes.title: Geometric and Generative State Modeling
themes.body: Innovative mathematical representations improve state analysis, using normalizing flows for continuous-variable phase-space tomography and a unified tensor in Liouville space to model directional spin relaxation.

connections.body: Both papers target practical multi-node quantum architectures: while the neutral-atom roadmap emphasizes inter-processor networking, the shuffling protocol provides circuit-agnostic entanglement purification to link them efficiently.
